title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 17, 2026 - Geopolitical Tensions & Tech Sell-off Weigh on Markets

items.headline: Wall Street Slumps as Chip Sell-off Overshadows Strong Earnings
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed lower on Thursday, July 17, 2026, with the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite experiencing the largest decline. A renewed sell-off in semiconductor stocks, despite some upbeat corporate earnings and stronger-than-expected economic data, dampened overall market sentiment. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East also contributed to a downbeat mood.

items.headline: UnitedHealth Group Beats Earnings
items.summary: UnitedHealth ($UNH) rose 1.2% after surpassing earnings estimates and raising its 2026 profit forecast, contributing to a climb in healthcare stocks.

items.headline: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals Leads Monthly Gainers
items.summary: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals ($CRNX) showed strong performance, leading as the best-performing stock in July 2026 with a significant monthly gain of 116.93% as of July 17.

items.headline: Semiconductor Stocks Slump
items.summary: The semiconductor sector experienced a significant sell-off. Arms Holdings saw a 5.41% drop, while Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) and Micron Technology ($MU) also declined over 5% each. SanDisk ($SNDK) nosedived over 12%. This broad-based sell-off was attributed to aggressive growth expectations in the industry.

items.headline: Netflix Tumbles on Weak Guidance
items.summary: Netflix ($NFLX) tumbled 8.92% in premarket trading after reporting mixed second-quarter financial results and issuing weak guidance for the third quarter.

items.headline: IBM Falls on Earnings Miss
items.summary: IBM ($IBM) stock tumbled 25% following preliminary earnings results for the second quarter, with sales lower than anticipated as customers shifted spending towards memory and storage products.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads Declines Amid Chip Sell-off
items.summary: Technology stocks led the market declines, with the sector falling 1.8% as semiconductor shares tumbled 4.3%. This sell-off in AI and semiconductor stocks was a primary driver behind the broader market's downtrend.

items.headline: Healthcare Sector Shows Resilience
items.summary: Despite broader market weakness, healthcare stocks climbed 2.2%, supported by strong earnings from companies like UnitedHealth Group.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Released
items.summary: Economic data painted a mixed picture. US retail sales rose modestly in June, jobless claims declined, and manufacturing activity in the Northeast strengthened. However, the housing sector remained under pressure as pending home sales fell more than expected, and homebuilder sentiment weakened due to elevated borrowing costs and affordability concerns.

items.headline: Fed Watch: Interest Rates Unchanged, Hike Expected Later in 2026
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) kept the target range for the federal funds rate unchanged at 3.50–3.75%. Market expectations have shifted, with a rate hike and potentially a second, expected by late 2026.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports from Q2 2026
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season is in full swing. TSMC reported a 77% jump in quarterly profit and raised its capital expenditure outlook, though its US-listed shares declined. UnitedHealth Group beat estimates. Netflix, however, provided weak Q3 guidance.
